title: Ember & Oak wood-fired restaurant sizzle reel: warm-graded sizzle cut with 9:16 + 1:1 reframes, menu title-card / price-callout graphic plates, hero cover thumbnail, and an animated logo end-card

source.reference: Restaurant Social Media Reel / Food Video Editor

grounding_note: VERBATIM intent from the real Upwork brief 'Restaurant Social Media Reel / Food Video Editor': a restaurant wants a short, fast, appetising social reel cut from raw food b-roll and kitchen footage; deliverables = a mouth-watering sizzle edit graded warm and 'appetising', with menu-item title cards and price callouts and a logo end-card, exported as a vertical 9:16 reel plus a 1:1 square and a hero cover thumbnail, ready for Instagram / TikTok / Facebook, on a lean budget and a quick turnaround. REASONABLY ENRICHED for a runnable Adobe-connector benchmark and tagged honestly with a FICTIONAL IP-clean brand (Ember & Oak, a wood-fired neighbourhood grill; no real trademark). The open 'send me your footage' bundle is fixed to concrete generatable inputs (four raw 16:9 dish/kitchen b-roll clips, one flat ungraded plated-hero still, a flat brand-logo PNG, a menu-callout CSV, and a brand-kit/reel brief). Because the Adobe connector surface has HARD LIMITS the spec respects and states up front: (1) NO connector tool color-grades moving video, so the brief's 'warm / appetising grade' is realized genuinely on the still-image cover/thumbnail via a real headless [C] raster chain, while video_create_quick_cut is only STEERED by a warm-appetising prompt and the actual footage grade (Lumetri/LUT) is the flagged manual Premiere finish; (2) the connector does NOT composite text/graphics onto video frames, so the menu title cards, price callouts and logo end-card are delivered as STANDALONE editable graphic plates / an animated Express graphic the editor keys onto the timeline; (3) video_resize is a same-length reframe (no trimming), the three video pieces are one Quick Cut master plus two reframes; (4) no generative fill / generative-expand, no text-to-image or from-scratch art, no upscaling, no AI object-removal-with-reconstruction is used — image_remove_background is alpha-only, image_vectorize traces a client-supplied flat logo, and the title cards are an authored-template data merge from the client's CSV, not free-form generative design.
one_line_ask: Turn Ember & Oak's four raw dish/kitchen b-roll clips, a flat plated-hero still, a flat logo, a menu-callout CSV and a brand brief into a fast, mouth-watering wood-fired sizzle reel — an AI sizzle-cut master plus 9:16 and 1:1 reframes, a warm-graded hero cover thumbnail, on-brand menu title-card / price-callout graphic plates, a vector logo and an animated logo end-card — the full editable-asset set delivered in the Creative Cloud project folder for the manual Premiere finish.
full_brief:
Ember & Oak is a fictional wood-fired neighbourhood grill, and we need a short-form social reel that makes people hungry the second it starts. This is for Instagram Reels, TikTok and Facebook. We want it fast, warm and mouth-watering — think tight cuts, live fire, steam, a cheese pull, a chocolate pour — with our menu items and prices flashing up and our logo landing at the end. Lean budget, quick turnaround.

Source material we're supplying: (1) four raw 16:9 clips straight off the camera — a wood-fired ribeye being sliced with steam rising, live flames on the grill as the chef lays steaks down, a fork twirling truffle rigatoni with a sauce/cheese pull, and warm chocolate poured over a molten dessert; every clip is flat and slightly under-exposed / cool straight out of camera and needs to be warmed up to look appetising; (2) one plated hero still of the ribeye on our dark oak table, also flat and slightly tilted, to become the cover/thumbnail; (3) our logo as a flat single-colour PNG; (4) a menu-callout CSV with the four dishes, their prices and a one-line descriptor; and (5) a brand-kit / reel brief with our colours, font feel, tagline, the on-screen hook line, the CTA and hashtags.

What we need done, mapped to Adobe Creative Cloud connector tools with every step honestly tagged for execution mode:

A. VIDEO — (1) summarise the four raw clips so we agree on the strongest moments and the sizzle order before cutting; (2) produce the master: one fast ~22s AI sizzle cut of the four clips, steered toward warm, appetising, high-energy pacing; (3) reframe that master to a 9:16 vertical reel and a 1:1 square, each SAME length (a reframe, not a re-trim); (4) QC the vertical reel's dimensions / duration / codec before handoff. NOTE up front: the actual warm colour grade on the moving footage is a manual Premiere/Lumetri finish and is OUT OF SCOPE for the automated connector pass — the connector delivers the sizzle assembly, the reframes and everything below.

B. HERO COVER / THUMBNAIL + THE WARM LOOK — from the flat plated-hero still, build the warm, appetising cover the reel opens on and that also locks the look the editor grades the footage toward: auto-tone baseline, push the white balance warm/golden, lift the highlights so the food glistens, boost the warm red/orange food tones, add overall vibrance, then select just the plated dish and pop it against the background, lay a subtle warm ember wash over the whole frame, and crop clean to a 1080x1350 portrait cover.

C. GRAPHICS — the on-brand overlays the editor keys onto the timeline (standalone plates / an animated graphic, NOT composited onto the video by us): (1) cut the plated dish out to a clean transparent PNG for the hero title card; (2) vectorise the logo to a scalable SVG for the end-card and corner bug; (3) pick on-brand display + body fonts; (4) author the title-card + end-card templates and data-merge the four menu rows (dish, price, descriptor) into per-item title-card / price-callout plates over our wood-texture background; (5) animate the logo end-card (logo reveal + CTA) into a short motion graphic.

D. BACKGROUND + DELIVERY — license a warm wood-grain texture for the card and end-card backgrounds, then deliver the full editable-asset set — all three video pieces, the cover thumbnail, the menu title-card plates, the logo SVG, the dish cutout and the animated end-card — in the Creative Cloud project folder ready for the manual Premiere finish. Two rounds of feedback should be plenty.

inputs.name: raw_ribeye_broll.mp4
inputs.gen_prompt: Raw 16:9 landscape food b-roll, 1920x1080, ~8 seconds, extreme macro close-up of a wood-fired ribeye steak resting on a dark oak carving board, a sharp knife slowly slicing through to reveal a juicy medium-rare pink interior, real steam curling up, glistening rendered fat, shallow depth of field with a slow gentle push-in, warm restaurant low light but shot deliberately FLAT and slightly under-exposed / slightly cool straight out of camera, candid documentary food-cinematography look, no captions, no text overlays, no logo, not glossy CGI
inputs.realism_notes: Ungraded/flat and slightly cool on purpose so the warm-look grade has real work to do (the moving-footage grade itself is the flagged manual finish; the still/thumbnail chain proves the look). A clear single hero dish so Quick Cut has a strong beauty-moment to select.

inputs.name: raw_grill_flames.mp4
inputs.gen_prompt: Raw 16:9 landscape kitchen b-roll, 1920x1080, ~7 seconds, live-fire grill action: tall orange flames leaping up as a chef's hands with metal tongs lay two steaks onto the glowing grate, sparks and embers flying, smoke drifting, dramatic high-contrast fire light against a dark kitchen, slight handheld drift, real motion blur, candid, no text overlays, no logo
inputs.realism_notes: High-energy motion + fire gives the sizzle cut a visually distinct dramatic beat to intercut with the calmer dish macros.

inputs.name: raw_pasta_pull.mp4
inputs.gen_prompt: Raw 16:9 landscape food b-roll, 1920x1080, ~6 seconds, macro shot of a fork lifting and twirling a nest of truffle rigatoni out of a bowl, glossy cream-and-truffle sauce clinging, a stretchy cheese/sauce pull, light steam, warm plate reflections, shallow depth of field, flat ungraded slightly cool color straight off camera, candid food-cinematography, no text, no logo
inputs.realism_notes: The cheese/sauce pull is a classic appetising 'stopping' moment for the sizzle cut; flat/cool grade so the warm-look treatment reads as a real correction.

inputs.name: raw_dessert_pour.mp4
inputs.gen_prompt: Raw 16:9 landscape food b-roll, 1920x1080, ~6 seconds, macro shot of warm glossy chocolate sauce being poured over a molten chocolate dessert on a small plate, the dessert cracking open as the sauce cascades and the molten centre oozes out, a dusting of cocoa, warm dim restaurant lighting shot flat and slightly under-exposed, shallow depth of field, candid, no text, no logo
inputs.realism_notes: A decadent closing beauty-moment for the sizzle cut; deliberately flat/dim so the warm grade and highlight-lift on the still cover have something to fix.

inputs.name: plated_hero_still.png
inputs.gen_prompt: Photoreal food-photography still, 1600x2000 portrait, a single plated wood-fired ribeye on a dark oak restaurant table shot slightly from above, charred crust and pink slices fanned out, a sprig of rosemary and flaky salt, a little negative space at the top for a title/logo, deliberately FLAT and slightly under-exposed with a slightly cool/dull white balance and a subtle ~2-degree tilt as if grabbed quickly on set, real food texture, real plate reflections, shallow depth of field, no text, no logo, no watermark
inputs.realism_notes: Deliberately flat, cool-WB and slightly tilted so image_apply_auto_tone, image_adjust_color_temperature (warm), highlight-lift and the vibrance/single-color chain all have genuine work; one clear plated-dish subject so image_select_by_prompt (dish pop) and image_remove_background (dish cutout) are meaningful. Photoreal => gpt-image-2.

inputs.name: ember_oak_logo.png
inputs.gen_prompt: Flat minimalist restaurant logo on a fully transparent background: the wordmark 'EMBER & OAK' in a warm high-contrast geometric serif above a small emblem of a single flame rising from an oak leaf, one solid warm ember-orange color (#C8531B), crisp clean edges, perfectly flat with no gradients and no photographic texture, centered, 2400x1400 PNG with alpha
inputs.realism_notes: Flat single-color art with crisp edges and alpha so image_vectorize produces a clean scalable SVG and it composites as a corner bug / end-card mark. Text-bearing brand art => gemini-3-pro-image.

inputs.name: menu_callouts.csv
inputs.gen_prompt: CSV with columns order,dish,price,descriptor,clip holding four Ember & Oak menu callouts mapped to the four b-roll clips, e.g. 1,'Wood-Fired Ribeye','$42','48-hr dry-aged, live-fire seared','raw_ribeye_broll.mp4'; 2,'Charred Grill Flight','$36','chef''s live-fire selection','raw_grill_flames.mp4'; 3,'Truffle Rigatoni','$26','fresh pasta, black truffle cream','raw_pasta_pull.mp4'; 4,'Molten Chocolate Bomb','$14','warm dark chocolate, sea salt','raw_dessert_pour.mp4'.
inputs.realism_notes: The variable-data source for the title-card / price-callout data merge (fill_text + document_render_layout) — one branded plate per dish. Not exact-timestamp cut instructions.

inputs.name: reel_brief.txt
inputs.gen_prompt: A short brand-kit / reel brief plain-text note for Ember & Oak: brand palette with hex (ember orange #C8531B, charred charcoal #211A15, warm cream #F3E7D3, oak brown #6B4A2B); font feel (a warm high-contrast display serif for dish names, a clean humanist sans for prices and descriptors); tagline 'Cooked over live fire.'; the on-screen opening hook line 'THIS IS FIRE.'; call-to-action 'Book your table — link in bio'; hashtags #EmberAndOak #WoodFired #EatLocal; tone note ('fast, warm, mouth-watering, ~20-25s, punchy, appetising'); and an explicit note that the final warm colour grade on the moving footage is the editor's manual Premiere finish.
inputs.realism_notes: Free-text brand/tone steering for font_recommend, the Quick Cut prompt, the color-grade targets and the graphic templates; also names the out-of-scope manual-finish item (the moving-footage grade) so the connector scope stays honest.

outputs.name: emberandoak_sizzle_master_16x9.mp4
outputs.spec: Primary deliverable: a fast ~22s AI sizzle-cut master assembled from the four raw clips, MP4 (H.264 target), 1920x1080 (16:9), steered toward warm, appetising, high-energy pacing (video_create_quick_cut). Exact final duration/spec are [A] async product-widget-polled targets, not fully headless-retrievable. The warm colour grade on the moving footage is the flagged manual Premiere/Lumetri finish and is NOT part of this automated deliverable.

outputs.name: emberandoak_reel_9x16.mp4
outputs.spec: 9:16 vertical reframe of the sizzle master, 1080x1920, SAME length as the master (video_resize reframe, no trimming), for Instagram Reels / TikTok / Facebook Reels. [A] async.

outputs.name: emberandoak_square_1x1.mp4
outputs.spec: 1:1 square reframe of the sizzle master, 1080x1080, SAME length as the master (video_resize reframe, no trimming), for in-feed placements. [A] async.

outputs.name: emberandoak_hero_thumbnail_1080x1350.png
outputs.spec: Warm, appetising hero cover / thumbnail, PNG 1080x1350 (4:5 portrait): the flat plated-hero still auto-toned, warmed toward golden, highlight-lifted so the food glistens, warm red/orange food tones boosted, vibrance added, the plated dish selected and popped against the background, a subtle warm ember wash applied, then cropped clean to 1080x1350. This is a straight crop/reframe of the existing still (no canvas extension or padding) and also locks the warm look the editor grades the footage toward.

outputs.name: dish_cutout.png
outputs.spec: Transparent plated-dish cutout (image_remove_background, alpha-only — background made transparent, no reconstruction) from the hero still, PNG with alpha, for placement inside the hero title-card plate.

outputs.name: menu_title_cards.png
outputs.spec: A set of four on-brand menu title-card / price-callout graphic plates (PNG), one per dish, produced by authoring a title-card template and data-merging the menu_callouts.csv rows (dish name + price + one-line descriptor) over the licensed wood-texture background, with the dish cutout on the hero card (fill_text + document_render_layout). Standalone editable overlay plates the editor keys onto the reel timeline — the connector does NOT composite text onto the video frames.

outputs.name: emberandoak_logo.svg
outputs.spec: Clean vectorized SVG of the client-supplied flat logo (image_vectorize) for the end-card and corner bug at any scale.

outputs.name: emberandoak_endcard_animated
outputs.spec: A short animated logo end-card graphic (animate_design) built from the authored Express end-card frame — logo reveal + tagline 'Cooked over live fire.' + CTA over the wood texture. Delivered as a standalone animated graphic the editor drops on the timeline close; the exact placement/timing on the timeline is part of the manual finish.

connector_workflow.note: Create a managed Creative Cloud project folder ('EmberAndOak_Sizzle_Reel') to hold every source and produced asset. Root of the deliverable package.
Upload raw_ribeye_broll.mp4 (media_type video/mp4) into the project folder as a real CC video asset to feed the sizzle chain.
Upload raw_grill_flames.mp4 into the project folder as a CC video asset.
Upload raw_pasta_pull.mp4 into the project folder as a CC video asset.
Upload raw_dessert_pour.mp4 into the project folder as a CC video asset. Steps 2-5 give video_create_quick_cut its four source assetIds.
Upload plated_hero_still.png (media_type image/png) into the project folder to drive the warm-look raster chain and the dish cutout.
Upload ember_oak_logo.png into the project folder so it can be vectorized for the end-card and corner bug.
Upload menu_callouts.csv (media_type text/csv) into the project folder as the variable-data source for the title-card / price-callout merge.

Upload reel_brief.txt into the project folder as the brand-kit / tone steering used by font_recommend, the Quick Cut prompt, the grade targets and the graphic templates. Steps 1-9 stand up the project with every source file ingested.

License and download a warm wood-grain / oak-plank texture still from Adobe Stock into the project folder to use as the on-brand background for the menu title cards and the end-card (the 'Oak' in the brand).

Summarize the four raw clips (content / strongest beauty-moments / logical sizzle order) so the order is agreed before cutting, steered by the reel_brief tone. Consumes the four ingested clips + brief. [A] async product action. (Content/flow shot-inventory pass over the visual footage.)

AI sizzle/highlight assembly across the four finalized clip assets (steps 2-5), target_duration ~22s, user_prompt for fast, warm, appetising, high-energy pacing steered by the step-11 summary + reel_brief. Produces emberandoak_sizzle_master_16x9.mp4 (16:9). [A] async — the product widget polls/notifies. The actual warm colour grade on the footage remains the flagged manual finish.
Reframe the sizzle master from step 12 to a clean 9:16 vertical 1080x1920, SAME length (no trim). Produces emberandoak_reel_9x16.mp4. [A] async.
Reframe the same sizzle master (step 12) to a 1:1 square 1080x1080, SAME length (no trim). Produces emberandoak_square_1x1.mp4. [A] async.
Read back the 9:16 reel's metadata (dimensions 1080x1920, duration, codec/container) to QC the reframe before handoff. Consumes step 13. [C] headless.
Lay down an auto-tone baseline on the flat, under-exposed plated-hero still (step 6) as a clean base for the warm grade. [C].
Push the cool/dull white balance warm and golden for the appetising Ember & Oak look. Consumes step 16. [C].
Lift the highlights so the rendered fat, sauce and steam glisten. Consumes step 17. [C].
Targeted boost of the warm red/orange food tones (the seared crust and pink slices) without over-saturating the whole frame. Consumes step 18. [C].
Add overall vibrance so the whole plate reads rich and appetising. Consumes step 19. [C].
Select just the plated dish ('the plated steak on the board') to drive a selective pop. Consumes step 20 and returns a maskURI. [C].
Apply a brightness/contrast lift ONLY within the dish mask from step 21 (real selective edit) on the vibrant image from step 20, so the food separates from the darker table. [C].
Lay a subtle warm ember (#C8531B) color wash over the whole frame to tie the cover to the brand palette. Consumes step 22. [C].

Crop/resize the graded still clean to the 1080x1350 (4:5) portrait cover, keeping the dish on a rule-of-thirds line and the top negative space for the title (a straight reframe of the existing frame, no canvas extension). Produces emberandoak_hero_thumbnail_1080x1350.png. [C].

Cut the plated dish out of the ingested hero still to a clean transparent PNG (alpha-only background removal — no reconstruction) for placement inside the hero title-card plate. Produces dish_cutout.png. [C].
Vectorize the flat logo PNG from step 7 into a clean scalable SVG (emberandoak_logo.svg) for the end-card and corner bug. [C].
Recommend on-brand type from the reel_brief tone: a warm high-contrast display serif for dish names and a clean humanist sans for prices/descriptors. Consumes the brief. [C].
Resolve the recommended families to concrete available Adobe Fonts assets (references the editor can use in the templates). Consumes step 27. [C].

Author the title-card and logo end-card as self-contained HTML (resolved fonts + logo SVG + wood-texture background + brand palette/CTA from the brief) and import to an editable Adobe Express document holding a title-card master frame and an end-card frame. Authored-template, not free-form generative design. [W] Express-widget.
Data-merge the four menu_callouts.csv rows (dish, price, descriptor) into the title-card template's text frames from step 29 — one populated card per dish. Authored-template variable fill. [T].

Render the four filled title-card / price-callout layouts to PNG plates, compositing the dish cutout (step 25) on the hero card over the wood texture. Produces menu_title_cards.png — standalone editable overlay plates (the connector does NOT composite text onto the video). [T] authored-template export.

Animate the end-card frame from step 29 (logo reveal + tagline + CTA over the wood texture) into a short animated logo end-card graphic. Produces emberandoak_endcard_animated. Standalone animated graphic for the timeline close; its placement/timing on the timeline is the manual finish. [W] Express-widget.

chaining_note: One continuous output-to-input chain anchored on the client-supplied assets. Steps 1-10 stand up the project: create the folder (1), upload the four clips (2-5), the hero still (6), the logo (7), the menu CSV (8) and the brand brief (9), then license the wood-texture background (10). Three sub-chains fan out from the ingested assets. (VIDEO [A]) media_summarize(11) consumes the four clips + brief and its footage_summary steers video_create_quick_cut(12) into the ~22s sizzle master; video_resize(13) and video_resize(14) each consume that master to produce the same-length 9:16 reel and 1:1 square; video_metadata(15) reads the 9:16 reel back for QC. (STILL/COVER [C]) a nine-link warm grade threads the hero still through every step — apply_auto_tone(16)->adjust_color_temperature(17)->adjust_highlights(18)->adjust_single_color_saturation(19)->adjust_vibrance_and_saturation(20); select_by_prompt(21) makes the dish mask, and both the vibrant image(20) and that mask(21) feed the masked adjust_brightness_and_contrast dish-pop(22)->apply_color_overlay(23)->crop_and_resize(24)=hero_thumbnail; separately remove_background(25) consumes the ingested still for the dish cutout and vectorize(26) consumes the ingested logo for the SVG. (GRAPHICS [C]/[W]/[T]) font_recommend(27) reads the brief -> find_fonts(28) resolves families -> export_html_to_express(29) authors the title-card + end-card templates from the resolved fonts + logo SVG(26) + wood texture(10); fill_text(30) merges the menu CSV(8) into that template; document_render_layout(31) renders the filled cards to PNG plates with the dish cutout(25) over the wood texture; animate_design(32) animates the end-card frame(29) with the logo SVG(26). All produced deliverables (12,13,14,24,31,26,25,32) land in the Creative Cloud project folder from step 1 for the manual Premiere finish. Every post-ingest step sets inputs_from to the producing step's output; nothing after step 10 starts from scratch.
difficulty_rationale: Long-horizon flagship: 32 connector calls, 24 distinct Adobe tools, spanning four connector groups and all four honest execution modes — [C] headless (asset ops, the nine-link raster grade, cutout, vectorize, fonts, video metadata QC), [A] async (footage summarize, Quick Cut, two reframes), [W] Express-widget (HTML->Express authoring, animate_design) and [T] authored-template (variable-data fill + layout render) — with three parallel sub-chains feeding the editable-asset set for the Premiere handoff, well past the >=20 calls / >=9 distinct-tool bar. The genuine creative spine is the nine-step headless [C] warm-grade cover chain with a real masked selective edit (select_by_prompt -> masked brightness/contrast dish pop) and a data-merge graphics sub-chain (fonts -> authored template -> CSV fill -> render -> animate). Difficulty comes from breadth, the strict output->input dependency, mixing all four modes, and faithfully re-scoping a Premiere-class food-reel brief onto the real Adobe connector surface. The spec is honestly mode-tagged and states its limits up front: NO connector tool color-grades the moving footage (the warm grade is proven on the still cover; the footage grade is the flagged manual Lumetri finish), the connector does NOT composite text onto video (menu title cards, price callouts and the logo end-card are delivered as standalone plates / an animated graphic the editor keys on), video_resize is same-length reframe, image_remove_background is alpha-only, and the title cards are an authored-template data merge from the client's CSV — so nothing on the not-available list is claimed as a core deliverable.

reverify.banned_capability_check: Confirmed NONE of the banned capabilities are used. No generative fill / generative-expand / outpaint / canvas extension: image_crop_and_resize (step 24) only crops/resizes the existing still down to 1080x1350, and video_resize (13,14) is a same-length reframe — no padding, no new pixels invented. No text-to-image / from-scratch art / bespoke logo or letterform design: the logo is client-supplied and only vectorized (image_vectorize, step 26) and the menu title cards are an authored-template variable-data merge from the client's menu_callouts.csv (fill_text + document_render_layout, steps 30-31) over a licensed stock texture, not free-form generative design. No upscaling / super-resolution. No AI object-removal-with-reconstruction: image_remove_background (step 25) is alpha-only (background made transparent, nothing reconstructed behind the subject). image_select_by_prompt (21) + masked image_adjust_brightness_and_contrast (22) is a real selective edit. Video steps are honest [A] async product actions: video_create_quick_cut (12) is visual-engagement AI cutting steered by free-text prompt/summary (not timestamp trimming), video_resize (13,14) is same-length 9:16 / 1:1 reframe (width/height only), media_summarize (11) is a visual content/flow shot-inventory pass over the footage. CRITICAL honesty points stated throughout: (a) NO connector tool color-grades moving video — the warm 'appetising grade' is realized only on the still cover/thumbnail via the [C] raster chain, while Quick Cut is merely prompt-steered and the footage Lumetri grade is the flagged manual finish; (b) the connector does NOT composite text/graphics onto video frames — the menu title cards, price callouts and logo end-card are standalone editable plates / an animated Express graphic the editor keys onto the timeline. export_html_to_express (29) and animate_design (32) are [W] Express-widget authoring of an editable design, not headless-retrievable files. asset_license_and_download_stock (10) licenses a real Adobe Stock texture. No Firefly/text-to-image generation, no external unreachable account, no Canva tools; every step server=='adobe'.
reverify.notes: All 32 steps are real Adobe-connector actions drawn only from the approved tool set (asset_create_folders, asset_add_file, asset_license_and_download_stock, media_summarize, video_create_quick_cut, video_resize, video_metadata, image_apply_auto_tone, image_adjust_color_temperature/highlights/single_color_saturation/vibrance_and_saturation/brightness_and_contrast, image_select_by_prompt, image_apply_color_overlay, image_crop_and_resize, image_remove_background, image_vectorize, font_recommend, find_fonts, export_html_to_express, fill_text, document_render_layout, animate_design) with a coherent output->input chain. Counts verified: connector_workflow has 32 steps -> tool_call_count=32; asset_add_file appears 8x and video_resize 2x but each counts once -> 24 distinct adobe-server tools = distinct_adobe_tools (>=9). Modes span [C]/[A]/[W]/[T]. Slot parity: category=video_edit, slot_id=M07 (next free motion slot after M01-M06), output_modality=video, workflow_nature=edit (transforms supplied footage/assets into edited deliverables), vertical=food_beverage. Grounded in the real Upwork 'Restaurant Social Media Reel / Food Video Editor' brief with a fictional IP-clean brand (Ember & Oak). media_summarize on food b-roll is framed as a visual content/flow shot-inventory pass, consistent with the acknowledged pattern in sibling motion specs.
